body.answer: Electricians get the cheapest leads of any trade — and Local Services Ads are the best deal in the building.
body.html: If you're an electrician shopping for leads, you're in luck: your trade has the lowest cost of the major trades. Google's Local Services Ads (LSA) run about $39 per lead, book 43.4% of them, on a $1,434 average ticket, for 8.52x ROAS (SearchLight Digital 2026). Those leads are exclusive — they come to you alone. That's the deal to beat.

body.h2: Why electricians pay less than HVAC and plumbing
body.answer: Your $39 LSA lead is cheaper than plumbing's $57 and HVAC's $51 — but your ticket is smaller, so every booked job has to count.
body.html:
Here's the spread on LSA cost per lead by trade (SearchLight Digital 2026): HVAC $51, Plumbing $57, Electrical $39. You pay the least to get the phone to ring. Nice.

But look at the other side. HVAC averages a $2,110 ticket and plumbing $1,714, while electrical sits at $1,434 (SearchLight Digital 2026). Your job is smaller. That means you can't afford to waste leads the way a guy selling a full HVAC system can. A cheap lead that never books is still money out the door.

body.h2: Search ads cost more than LSA for you
body.answer: A Google Search lead runs you about $58 — half again pricier than the $39 you'd pay on LSA.
body.html:
Local Services Ads aren't the only way to buy on Google. There are also regular Search ads — the text ones at the top. For electricians, a Search lead costs about $58 (LocaliQ 2025), versus $39 on LSA (SearchLight Digital 2026).

So same search engine, same customer, and the LSA version is cheaper. LSA also shows your reviews and a Google badge right up top, and you only pay when someone actually contacts you. For a smaller-ticket trade, that's the spot to start before you spend a dime on Search.

body.h2: Exclusive beats shared — especially on a small ticket
body.answer: LSA leads are yours alone; shared-lead sites sell the same customer to 2-5 pros, and a small ticket can't survive that fight.
body.html:
This is the part that matters most for electricians. LSA leads are exclusive — one lead, one pro, you (2026). Compare that to the shared-lead world: Thumbtack sells a lead to 4-5 pros and Angi to 2-4 (2026). You're racing three other electricians to the same person.

Why does this hurt you more than the big-ticket trades? Because your job is worth $1,434 (SearchLight Digital 2026), not $2,110. When you split your odds across a crowd of bidders, you're paying to lose most of the time on a job that didn't have much fat to begin with. The math on shared leads gets ugly fast for a smaller ticket.

It shows up in the booking too: shared leads convert at just 6-10%, while a direct phone call closes up to 40% (2026). Exclusive gets you closer to that high end.

body.h2: Speed is the cheapest edge you've got
body.answer: Most customers hire whoever answers first, so a fast callback is free money on top of a good lead.
body.html:
You don't have to spend more to win more — you have to answer faster. 78% of customers hire the first contractor who responds (Lead Connect 2026), and replying within five minutes makes you 100x more likely to land the job than waiting thirty (MIT Sloan 2026).

That's the multiplier on your $39 lead. An exclusive LSA lead that you call back in two minutes is worth a lot more than a cheaper lead you get to an hour later. Meanwhile, 14% of calls to contractors go unanswered (CallRail 2026) — every one of those is a booked job somebody else took.

body.answer: Electricians need the fewest reviews of any trade to rank on the Maps page — a low bar worth clearing.
body.html:
Reviews decide who gets picked. 91% of people read local reviews and most won't consider a business under 4 stars (BrightLocal 2025), and 81% lean on Google reviews specifically (CallRail 2026).

Good news again for electricians: the bar to rank is low for your trade. The median Google review count for contractors ranking on page one of Google Maps is just 64 for electrical — versus 337 for plumbing and 519 for HVAC (Booked Job GBP scrape, 2026, major TX metros). You can climb the map with far fewer reviews than the other trades. Ask every customer. It's the cheapest marketing you'll ever do.

faq.q: What's the cheapest way for an electrician to buy leads?
faq.a: Google Local Services Ads. For electricians they run about $39 per lead and book 43.4% of them at 8.52x ROAS (SearchLight Digital 2026) — the lowest cost per lead of the major trades, and the leads are exclusive to you.

faq.q: Are shared leads like Angi or Thumbtack worth it for electricians?
faq.a: They're a tougher fit for a smaller ticket. Thumbtack sells each lead to 4-5 pros and Angi to 2-4 (2026), and shared leads convert at only 6-10% versus up to 40% for a direct call (2026). On a $1,434 average ticket (SearchLight Digital 2026), splitting your odds gets expensive fast.

faq.q: Why are electrician leads cheaper than HVAC or plumbing?
faq.a: Demand and ticket size. Electrical LSA leads cost $39 versus $51 for HVAC and $57 for plumbing, but the average electrical job is $1,434 versus $2,110 for HVAC (SearchLight Digital 2026). You pay less per lead, but each job is smaller — so booking rate and fast response matter even more.

title: The Best Leads for Electricians: Why LSA Wins (2026 Numbers)
h1: The Best Leads for Electricians: Why Local Services Ads Win
meta: Electricians get the cheapest leads of any trade. Google LSA runs ~$39/lead at 8.52x ROAS (SearchLight 2026). Why exclusive beats shared on a smaller ticket.
short: Electricians get the cheapest leads of any trade. Google Local Services Ads run about $39 per lead, book 43.4% of them on a $1,434 average ticket, and return 8.52x ROAS (SearchLight Digital 2026). The leads are exclusive to you. On a smaller ticket, that exclusivity is exactly what makes them the best buy.
statLabel: Cost per electrical LSA lead — the cheapest of the trades, at 8.52x ROAS (SearchLight Digital 2026)

_verdict.notes: Every numeric and dollar claim is supported by the approved list with correct source attribution and year. Verified: electrical LSA $39 / 43.4% book / $1,434 ticket / 8.52x ROAS (SearchLight Digital 2026); LSA CPL spread HVAC $51, Plumbing $57, Electrical $39 (SearchLight 2026); tickets HVAC $2,110, Plumbing $1,714, Electrical $1,434 (SearchLight 2026); electrical Search CPL ~$58 (LocaliQ 2025); Thumbtack 4-5 pros / Angi 2-4 (2026); shared leads 6-10% vs direct call up to 40% (2026); 78% hire first responder (Lead Connect 2026); 5-min = 100x (MIT Sloan 2026); 14% missed calls (CallRail 2026); 91% read reviews / under 4 stars (BrightLocal 2025); 81% Google reviews (CallRail 2026); GBP review medians electrical 64 / plumbing 337 / HVAC 519 (Booked Job GBP scrape 2026). No fabricated statistics, no invented illustrative dollar amounts, no wrong source attributions. Note: the article labels the $58 LocaliQ figure a "lead"/"Search lead" cost while the approved list terms it "CPL" — same metric, not a discrepancy. Tone is casual and punchy ("Nice.", "free money", "cheapest marketing you'll ever do") but all rhetorical flourishes sit on top of accurate, sourced numbers; no overpromising or fabricated specificity.
